children.text: A software developer who writes about building things on the web. Based somewhere with good coffee and reliable internet.
Who we are
Our website address is: http://www.davidmulholland.net.au.
What personal data we collect and why we collect it
Comments
When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.
Media

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.
Contact forms
Cookies

If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.
If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.
Embedded content from other websites

Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.
Analytics
How long we retain your data

If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.
What rights you have over your data

If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.
Where we send your data
Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.
KEEP RATES LOW
FOCUS ON THE BASICS ROADS, FOOTPATHS, DRAINAGE, RUBBISH
SUPPORT FOR SMALL BUSINESS TO GET THROUGH COVID
Asking questions to ensure we get the best result from the North East Link
"We can do better!"
My name is DAVID MULHOLLAND.

My grounding in life comes from my Italian grandparents hard work ethic and love of gardening, my fathers entrepreneurial spirit and my mothers community spirit and volunteerism which earned her an OAM.
My wife Sophie and my young daughter Isabella live in Eltham for its beautiful environment and strong community spirit. Sophie and I understand the need for quality services for all ages.
Our streetscapes and bush setting are why we love Eltham and it must be protected. Living in the Green Wedge is special, it must be protected for future generations.
The Nillumbik Shire Council could consult better, keeping you informed on planning and major projects.
We need to protect against overdevelopment by setting a Neighbourhood Character Strategy which sets in stone how we want Eltham to be.
Nillumbik had a reputation for spiralling high Council rates. Whilst this trend has reversed in recent times, I will ensure this stays on track and I will maintain the policy of lower rates.
I will focus on getting the basic services right: rates, roads, fire prevention, drainage, footpaths, support for sporting and community groups, assisting business.

I fought for a North East Link and this is on its way. I will fight for local infrastructure issues that will make a difference to your day-to-day lives like our roads, new footpaths, protecting local amenity, improved parks and community facilities.

I am a Senior Manager at Deloitte Digital, one of the most preeminent IT companies in Australia.  I have worked with major businesses and government departments throughout Australia making the IT experience of their customers user-friendly.  We should be able to interact with our Council in a user-friendly manner with online messaging in addition to the option of a quick response on the phone.

I chair the Eltham & Nillumbik Neighbourhood Watch, Eltham Ratepayers Action Group, and am a member of Eltham Historical Society and Monty-Eltham RSL. I love Eltham and will ensure we get back to basics.
We can do better.
Vote 1 David Mulholland.
Like me on Facebook to follow my campaign
I want to hear your views
Fill in my survey and let me know the issues that matter to you
COMPLETE THE SURVEY
David, Sophie and Isabella at Christmas timeAdvocating on behalf of the communityDavid with Bella
I’m Associate Director, Service Experience & Design for the University of Melbourne managing service and digital design and transformation across the University.
I'm a generalist strategist, playing many different roles in large digital transformations.
Previously, I was a Senior Digital Product Manager for the University of Melbourne owning the unimelb.edu.au website navigation and Information Architecture.
Prior to that, I was Senior UX & Product Design Manager for Flybuys, Australia’s biggest loyalty program, based in Melbourne for 3 and 1/2 years.
Prior to that, I was a Senior Manager at Deloitte Digital working across product management, design leadership and managing cross-functional teams for a decade.

I am a leading user experience design leader, digital product strategist, business analyst and product manager. Over the past ten years in the industry I have led the design, functional and delivery streams in large digital transformation projects.
My philosophy
Function over form
I approach work with a strong philosophy of function over form.
My idols are Don Norman and Jakob Nielson and I live the philosophy in the two books ‘Design of Everyday Things’ by Don Norman and ‘Don’t Make me Think’ by Steve Krug’.
My primary goal in design is to ensure they can complete their task and be useful to their lives.
No matter how pixel-perfect your interface, you need to make sure it works.
Helpful
I find that a very common trait among designers I meet is that they want to help people.
I do too.
I have been leading experience design teams for over 8 years and my style is to coach, promote enquiry, teach proper design process and provide honest feedback.
I have daily sessions with my team and weekly 1-on-1’s to provide a connection and continuous learning culture.
Strategic
Everything must have a purpose and designed to meet an end goal.

I understand that to build a vision for a product, to create differentiation, we need a strategy to get there.  I understand that every change must make business sense.  I understand that we need justification for change highlighting the problems using data and ensuring ROI or efficiencies can be gained by making the change.
At Flybuys I have developed transformational strategy to CX of the entire company and developed DesignOps strategies to improve capabilities in our UX practice and create efficiencies.

Day to day within a product itself every design must have a strategy down to the smallest element of design. The simplest ensuring we are following design guidelines and when introducing changes, we are making a strategic decision to expand it for new designs.
Email David Mulholland
dave.o.mulholland@gmail.com
Message David Mulholland
David Mulholland | LinkedIn
Cr. Karen Egan, Mayor and small business owner

David is a real professional. He's a local who loves our community. He gets the needs of business and government. He's helped the Nillumbik landowners advocate against terrible planning policy which would put our Green Wedge at risk. Importantly he supports keeping rates low which other candidates don't support. We need young energetic people like him on Council.
Kirrily Romero, Small Business Owner

David Mulholland is the fresh blood we need in Nillumbik to help the Shire move forward progressively and sustainably into the future.
He understands it’s not one way or the other.
Dave will help us walk the fine line of protecting the environment we live in, while finding how to be economically sustainable in order to preserve the environment we have fiercely held for all these years.
Mary Wooldridge, Former Victorian Minister for Community Services and Mental Health

David Mulholland has the best interests of the community at heart. He has vast experience working with government, in business and as a common sense, community activist. David never hesitates to fight for community services and the environment. Residents will have every reason to be delighted to have David as their Councillor.
Brian Murray, Nillumbik Ratepayers Association

I offer support to David and his candidacy for Nillumbik Council. David combines the skills of important consultative principles, fair deliberation of issues and follows a strong family tradition of representative involvement in Local Government. David has committed to us that he will keep rates low.
Peter McWilliam, Board Member of the Northern Hospital, 30 year resident, CFA Volunteer
David will get things done in Eltham. He has the qualities and experience that will make a difference in our local area. David is a strong independent who will stand up for what matters.
Here is my BACK TO BASICS plan for Eltham, Nillumbik Shire, Wingrove Ward and as your elected representative I will implement these policies in the next four years.
Rates & Budget

Keep rates lowEnsure there is no wasteful spendingEnsure there is no self promotion of Councillors using ratepayer fundsYears of experience working with government enabling them to provide better cost to serve
Roads

Develop a traffic plan to deal with congestion particularly at peak hourDeliver complementary road projects to the North East LinkSupport a community consultative group to get the Fitzsimons Lane upgrade right to fix the traffic issues and so it doesn't destroy the Eltham Gateway Zone
Public Transport

Improvements to bus stops and additional sheltersAdvocate for a new Eltham station, parking and drop-off areasAdvocate for Diamond Street level crossing removal to be considered as part the Hurstbridge railway duplication
Graffiti
Remove tagging and graffiti immediately. This is unacceptable vandalism of public and private property and requires protections to stop reoccurrence
Fire

Clean up our roadsides and council reserves to reduce fire loadContinued support and assistance for our local SESInvestigate options to allow escape routes in case of fire.Support and invest in fire safety infrastructure like the Kangaroo Ground Fire Tower
Footpaths & Drainage
Create a stormwater management plan to overcome floodingBuild the missing stormwater and sewerage infrastructureImplement a footpath construction plan and remove the delays in repairs
Small Business

Provide rates waivers for businesses who have staff on JobKeeperProvide grants for digital services to assist small business transition to e-commerceEmploy a digital marketer to advertise local businessesSet up a digital e-commerce marketplace to enable local small businesses and individuals to sell products onlineActively clean major touchpoints in Eltham to prevent COVID outbreaks
Planning

Negotiate planning outcomes that will reflect what we expect as appropriate for ElthamSet aside a fund to represent the community at VCAT with professional legal counselImproved communication with the ratepayers of whats going onEnsure that landowner rights are protected and that planning scheme amendments is protected and not overridden by State policy
Environment

Show climate leadership by supporting a solar farm for the Shire to support a low carbon futureInvest in solar panels on the Eltham Leisure Centre to take it off gridDevelop a walking and bridges strategy for Eltham South to consider trails and connections across Diamond Creek, Yarra River and Monsalvat to reflect rural character.Increase investment and support management in the protection and improvement of Yarra River, Diamond Creek, Edendale Farm, Lenister Farm, Alistair Knox Park, Linear Reserve and all of our natural assets.Implement Eltham Lower Park Master PlanSupport upgrades to pocket parks
Community

Build a Skate Park in ElthamUpgrade play equipment across Eltham while ensuring any of the safe replaced equipment is donated to community groups.Support community facilities like Mens Shed, Eltham Library, Old Shire Offices and Old Eltham CourthouseIncreased opening hours at the Eltham LibraryNew bike paths for Eltham
Rubbish
Provide residents choice to have a tip "voucher" to allow residents to either deliver trailer loads of hard waste and green waste to the transfer station or have it collected from kerbside.
Managing state issues
Fitzsimmons Lane UpgradeCommunity Hospital on Old Shire Office Site and War MemorialFour storey car park at Eltham

data.title: Hello. I'm David Mulholland, a Product & Design Leader

children.text: I'm a Product and Design Leader located in Melbourne, where I transform digital products and services.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RVTu2PMqhj0
Venus Liu, Product Designer @ Flybuys

David Mulholland is an exceptional leader. David has been instrumental in introducing and providing learning opportunities through the Nielsen Norman Group UX certification, ensuring our team stays at the forefront of industry standards. Moreover, David encourages us to challenge ourselves and push our boundaries. His support and motivation have been crucial in our professional development.

David is not only an outstanding leader but also an exceptional contributor. He has adeptly led numerous challenging conversations, always with a focus on constructive outcomes. His ability to navigate complex situations with clarity and poise is truly remarkable.
Working with David has been a truly enriching experience. His vision, commitment, and encouragement have made a significant impact on both our team and my personal growth.
Harley Giles, Chief Growth Officer, Flybuys

David's ability to embrace that type of change and the ambiguity that comes with that it's been really good.
I always find the output from David's work to be really thoughtful really insightful, and it's been really good.
Claudia Grinzi, Head of Member Operations

I worked with David for 12 months at Flybuys. When I joined the business in the new Head of Member Operations role, David warmly welcomed me and proactively shared relevant member research and insights that he and his Product Design team had produced, to assist me to better understand the member base and support me in my role.

David also led comprehensive discovery and design work on our member FAQs, which are a testament to the success of the platform with our members, supporting them to self-serve and to reduce service centre volumes.

David makes the complex simple and takes the time needed to truly understand the problem at hand before moving into solution mode.
He has a very clear, concise and engaging way of presenting and is a wonderful storyteller.
Tony Siyavong, Head of Product
It's been a privilege to work with David over a number of years in a Design, Product, and Technology trio where David has played a key role in designing improved experiences for customers.

David proactively brings insights from different industries to curate the right experiences for users to interact and engage with. He has led a team of designers that has brought to life key experiences like the Flybuys and Coles Digital Fuel Dockets that has been in the works for a number of years, the onboarding and linking experiences with key partners Bunnings, Officeworks, and OnePass, and a significant number of member self-serve capabilities that focused on key moments that matter.

As an experience leader that has been able to work cross functionally and maintained a balance between experience, technical solution, and business outcomes, David is a capable professional that can navigate through complex and large-scale initiatives with a proactive and forward-thinking mindset.
Sanika Dandekar, CX & Insights Specialist

David is a fantastic mentor and guide for me at Flybuys. He is approachable and open minded. He is a strong voice for the members and has managed many discovery projects effectively. He has led and executed multiple strategic initiatives across the Product design space. He has a holistic vision that unifies customer Research with product design to deliver relevant business goals. He is a fantastic asset to any team that he is a part of!
Yuna Hu, Senior Product Designer

I had the privilege of working with David during our time at Flybuys. He is not only a remarkably talented designer but also a skilled people manager. His ability to inspire and lead the team while delivering exceptional design work is truly commendable. David is a true asset to any organisation, and I highly recommend him as both a colleague and a leader.
Damian Fasciani, VP: Product, Expert360

In my first 6 months at Caydon, we embarked on building not just a technically proficient set of digital products, but a product set that delivers a world class experience. David was a key member of the Deloitte Digital team leading our UX design, while also adding valuable input into our technical integration strategy across our stack with AWS, Sitecore, Salesforce, and Okta.

David has great business sense, can dive into the technical detail while articulating the impact on design and UX in parallel. I've found David's working style to be both flexible and highly professional. His engagement style and communication is of a very high standard. David added a unique dimension to our digital projects, as we hit challenges and needed to pivot quickly he was able to break down our challenges and present multiple avenues that would lead to our overall business goals. A pleasure to work with.
More case studies on request.

When I started at Flybuys I leveraged the a design process to understand the painpoints our team was going through. Based on this I utilised the NNGroup process to develop a bespoke roadmap to solve these issues.
We mapped out all the pain points to strategic and tactical goals.  I developed a rough roadmap based on the outcomes.
It's great to see the achievements we made and the strides we have achieved through this initial roadmapping exercise which set the vision for the years ahead.

The first priority was rolling out from Sketch to Figma and consolidating key components and tokens into one Design System.  It was still inconsistent but it highlighted the opportunities to fix these problems.
Why Flybuys Needs a Design Management System

Over two decades and with multiple production teams, our interfaces, products, and pages have become inconsistent, making our products look unpolished. There's a clear disconnect between our web and app versions, showing differences in appearance, functionality, and capabilities.

We have numerous duplicate components with minor variations, such as styling and alignment differences, or unique functionalities that exist only in one version, like a table within a banner used just once. For example, the screen shows four slightly different banner components, but there are many more variations.
These small differences mean we’ve repeatedly designed and developed the same components, sometimes so many times that we lose count. The impact is significant, but we're working to change that.
Why We Need a Unified Design System

Our engineering teams currently manage styles like colors and typography—what we call tokens—in separate silos. Each platform (web, iOS, Android) has its own token library, which each team must manually update. This has led to inconsistencies and growing disparities over time.
We have muddled fonts, inconsistent spacing and alignment, and varying aesthetics across pages. Moreover, we've created duplicate components with minimal visual differences.
This fragmented approach has caused the issues we face today.
The Future with Our Design System
Now, all teams will access designs from a single token repository. This includes colours, typography, spacing, rounded corners, elevation, and shadow—everything that forms what I call Flybuys DNA.

This unified source is the heart of our design system. Beyond the DNA tokens, it includes principles, usage guidelines, and accessibility standards, ensuring everyone in the business can access this information.

Previously, any design change required manual updates by each team. Now, with our versioned and fluid design system, a change is made once and can be easily updated by downloading the latest file and dragging and dropping.
The Role of Our Design System

Our Design System is the starting point for every design at Flybuys. It unifies experiences for our members, showcases our partners, and ensures consistent functionality and aesthetics across all platforms.
We've built it to the finest details:

Colour Token System: Ready for dark mode and themes, tested in real-world situations. Previously, different versions of components and colors made maintenance tiresome. Now, everything is housed in our Design System and can be activated instantly.
Mobile-first Typography: Optimized for legibility and inclusiveness.
Grid and Spacing Framework: Ensures proper alignment and spacing for all uses now and in the future.

With about 15% of people worldwide having disabilities, and many more experiencing temporary or situational limitations, our Design System prioritizes accessibility. It incorporates left-to-right text, contrast requirements, and inclusive engineering to ensure usability and reliability for every Australian.
Benefits
How we measure value
Problem

Flybuys ambition is to become Australia’s leading loyalty experience; however, there are a number of challenges that need to be overcome to deliver a seamless instore redemption that is consistent across all partners.
There is a strong reliance on the physical card for in-store redemptions​
The Flybuys redemption experience lags competitors in digital and omni-channel experiences​
Members increasingly engage across multiple Flybuys partners and would prefer one way of redeeming points across partners​
Vision

Remove reliance on a physical card to increase engagement and grow redemption activities for both first time redeemers and the overall customer base.​
Create a seamless instore redemption experience that is at parity or better than competitors to exceed members' expectations. ​
Roll out one in-store redemption method for all partners, driving ease of use, and a more convenient and cohesive experience for members.
​Success Measures

Increasing spending ​at the store​
Reducing time ​at the till​
Mitigating the need ​for staff intervention​
Increased redemptions
Brand ​uplift​ / NPS
Reduce calls
Opportunities

Solving key member painpoint of requiring a physical card and the need to convert points to Flybuys Dollars
Simplify the program for a member with the phasing out of Flybuys Dollars and associated benefits with service costs(call centre)
Consistent redemption experience across all retail shareholder partners
Commercial benefit in redeeming points on member balances (circa $500m) through unlocking first time redeemers and increased frequency of redemption.
Increased program engagement and awareness with more members interacting in store with partners
Scale of experience in touching all members, irrespective of preference of communication channel
Risks

Complex to execute a single experience across shareholder partners
Requires changes to PoS systems, costly and time consuming for partners and leading to a slow engagement process for Flybuys
Requires prioritisation and commitment from senior leadership across brands
Flybuys support may be required to reimplement in case of future POS upgrades
The objectives of the program experience framework was to:
Create clarity of roles and responsibilities across program and brands
Ensure consistency of member experience across partners and partner channels
Minimise friction in member experience
Maintain integrity of program design and delivery
Facilitate the delivery of value for customers and shareholders

The flybuys team identified a need to enhance the existing mobile application to provide a foundation for gamified and targeted marketing and trade driving offer delivery. The focus was to use design, prototyping and customer testing to seek to validate the approach for future marketing campaigns. The key outcomes defined by flybuys leadership for this capability:
Drive trade – define and implement functionality that drives increased trade rather than activities focused on accelerated point capture or varying rewards;

Collect and validate email contact information – enable a non-intrusive mechanism to capture and/or validate email contact information by requiring members to supply their email address in order to play and win at the game or activity; and
Create customer stickiness – facilitate a new way to talk to customers and connect suppliers delivering loyalty across the included brands.
Flybuys wanted to focus on the following:
Creating a sense of adventure within the flybuys App and eliminate “zombie participants”, allowing users to take ownership of their own journey as they work toward a reward that suits them;
Providing a cumulative set of objectives, allowing customers to select the path they wish to follow to achieve their desired goal, imparting a sense of control;
Providing partners with greater exposure to key audiences by allowing them to surface their products in a method that encourages higher customer engagement;
Increasing visibility of partner products to those consumers deemed to have a higher propensity to purchase that brand;
Providing the opportunity for increased activity around key dates throughout the year, when spending typically rises; and

Establishing a reusable architecture that facilitates rapid campaign and communication capabilities. This would be supported by a campaign catalogue of templated solutions or bespoke capabilities to expedite time-to-market.

The design sprint is a step by step design system for solving big problems and testing big ideas and really it's being used a lot for building better products faster it was developed at Google first by Jake Knapp the guy who actually wrote this book as a way to bring design into Google in a way that was measurable in a way that was a little bit more logical and cold and specific than design thinking which is a bit more open and let's say creative.

The design sprint as a process that you can plug into any problem or any type of problem and run the process and really always make sure you're validating ideas make sure you're always solving these problems.
When we use a traditional software development lifecycle we would see that we would have these kind of typical development processes.

Whether it's this cycle of starting with an idea spending time designing and building and coming up with maybe multiple versions and then deciding which ones to do and then implementing and developing and building them, finally launching and then collecting information on what customers really thought about the idea.

It's like this classic lean startup loop where we thought  "we're going through this loop so it must be the most efficient way to do things" but what we normally see and when I'm running design sprints always getting lots and lots of nods with people when I bring this up.

What you normally see in these processes is much more of the implementation and the building phase stretching out longer and longer with miscommunications and misalignments and teams handing over and passing information back and forth from each other. The designers designing and developers saying that won't work and "let's go back to the drawing board".

The process usually ends up looking a lot more like this where you maybe don't even actually get to launch and don't ever even actually get to see that data and find out if the idea was even worthwhile and valid in the first place.

It would actually have an impact on your business closing this loop and making this loop a lot smaller validating and getting data on a product or idea before spending all this time. This is really what the design sprint is all about.

It's all about getting a group of people in the room a mixed team of people to work on an idea together all at the same time. You get the right group of people in the room and clear their calendars for the whole week so that they can work together on this problem.

For many organisations, we have a roadmap or a strategy and a delivery team.  When you have big vague challenges, you have all seen the value of the design sprint in coming up with a fantastic result which is user tested and proven and at this point or after further iteration can be slotted into a detailed design and build process.
Week 1 – Prepared, Stakeholder interviews
Week 2 – Design Sprint 2 day workshop, Prototyping
Week 3 – Prototyping, User Testing, Revision
Week 4 – Presentation, Prototype development for User Testing
Week 5 – Finalise based on feedback
In the sprint workshops we defined the problem as a group
We sketched
We each submitted a concept to be judged
We decided a concept
We presented back our votes to the decider
And we chose the Shake to Start concept
Then we collaborated to define the flow which users would take in the testing
And storyboarded the concept
Prototyped over 3 days.
We developed a working prototype in code
We validated it with customers
Data was captured in the other room. We analysed the results after each test and work out what we would iterate as a team.

There was high engagement by the users when the game was played. There were expressions of excitement when the offers were collected and also expressions of  frustration when the game was lost. One of the interviewer’s went ‘AAAARGH!!’ when he lost. 
All of the interviewees wanted to play the game more than once.  Players seemed hooked by the challenge of the game and the benefit of rewards.  
All of the interviewees noted that this is unique in the market. 
The simple game mechanic allowed for fast learning of the game.  The tilt to play mechanic and onboarding experience allowed users to learn the game fully by the second play through, with improved playability after four plays.

A long term levelling system to keep players coming back.  A status of where they are up to would allow for a longer term hook to the game.  Showing the rewards that are available could entice replayability.
A high quality of offers is required to keep players interested in this game. The investment of time by users gives them a high expectation of the rewards they will get.
The instructions leading into the game needs to be a frictionless process while educating the user of the game. Some users didn’t read the instructions but learned by playing.  A more intuitive tutorial was required for players to “get it” and get hooked.
In game incentives could be added to add an extra ‘hook’ which will keep customers coming back to the game.
What we did next
Feasibility
Hack week development to finalise prototype based on feedback
Creative refinement
Further user testing
Prioritised feature backlog

I developed and enhanced our discovery process at Flybuys which consists of several steps that aim to understand the user needs, design solutions, test and validate them, and deliver a high-quality product. This process is important because it helps us to create a user-centric product that meets the business goals and provides a great user experience.
Our approach

Every product is unique. They vary in complexity and risk, they can be new or an improvement, they can have technical constraints or unlimited possibilities, and they can have clear or vague requirements.
That's why we customise our Discovery process for each initiative at the beginning of the quarter.
We first define the product scope, the risk, make assumptions about how it might work and then evaluate what research is needed.
The goal is to deliver the best customer experience and reduce risk for the business.
Scoping

Here is an example of our tailored approach across three different initiatives.  Based on complexity, risk, if it’s a new product or enhancement and whether the requirements are clear or ambiguous we can estimate the length of discovery.
Case Study - New onboarding Experience

We kicked off the Onboarding Experience Discovery after findings from CX Insights that 39% mentioned they had some form of difficulty in the sign up process, 30% were not aware of the need to activate offers, 36% of app users mentioned they had some form of difficulty with the app and only 49% of users understand how to redeem Flybuys points.
Case Study - Digital Acquisition

We improved the in-store QR code scanning experience by using the product discovery process. We researched the customers' needs and challenges, and created an MVP solution. The solution collected more data, offers dynamic incentives, and simplifies digital onboarding for our members which boosted acquisition by 22%.
Case Study - Implement a new feature in the app

The goal was to implement the ability for multiple catalogues to be added to our app where only one currently exists.  We reviewed the existing design assets, our current design patterns to enable this and began to design concepts based on this.  As the User Interface is well defined, it is low risk and low complexity.
Core Responsibilities & Accountabilities
Management of design lifecycle for all digital channel capability, maximising customer centricity
Design System & associated lifecycle management
Detailed user journey mapping, IA and content creation
Interaction design, prototyping and usability testing
High-fidelity UX/UI design
Service Blueprints
Accessibility policy development and audit management
DesignOps process to enable rapid proto-typing and design discovery including tech feasibility
End-to-end experience design process and management of omni-channel experience
Design Principles
User Experience Governance
Accessibility framework  - accessibility policy, audit and team education
Planning and Process
Agile Fortnightly Planning
We work closely with our partners in the product teams, platform and marketing teams to balance priorities ongoing in an agile way.
Any changes to timelines are reflected and communicated in real time with Product Management present.
Quarterly Planning

Teams have visibility of who is working on what initiative and when.  The Product Design Roadmap is updated quarterly and adjusted in quarter to be proactive about knock on effects to future quarters. While we started with MIRO, this was transitioned into Monday over time.
Long term planning

I would work closely with the head of product, product managers and the Tech leadership team to ensure we can proactively deliver requirements to product and platform teams so they can build as planned.  This capacity planning meant we could accurately plan out months in advance knowing we had appropriate resources.  We could also raise the need for further resources or prioritisation against other initiatives early on where required.
Initial information gathering
User Research
Research methods conducted for collecting user insights
User Insights
Insights from the Customer Journey Mapping Research display:
A drop in the NPS score from +21 at the sign-up stage to +9 at the onboarding stage.
30% of the members are not aware of the need to activate offers.
Only 49% understand how to redeem Flybuys points.
22 % members had the perception that it takes too long to collect enough points to make the Flybuys program worthwhile.
42% members at the onboarding stage prefer the Flybuys app as a key channel for ongoing engagement.
Initiative

From the research, we learnt that members who have had a positive first experience are significantly more engaged with the program. Thus, we are focusing on increasing member engagement and reducing lapsing members by delivering a seamless initial onboarding experience.
Objectives
Motivating all members to engage regularly with Flybuys program
Creating awareness about the benefits of the Flybuys program
Providing an engaging initial experience to new members
How
How might we help new users efficiently get started and existing members actively engage with Flybuys?

We introduced the following 8 steps to tackle the problems that we learnt from our research. Members can access an onboarding tour via the ‘How it works’ page from the website and a new ‘Get started’ page in their account dashboard.
Page: How it works

After users signed in, the section ‘Ways to collect points’ will be replicated with a new section ‘onboarding tour’. Besides, the tour can be accessed via a new hyperlink ‘Get started’ on the username’s dropdown menu.
Page: Dashboard - Homepage
On the user dashboard homepage, a new section ‘onboarding tour” will be added above the section ‘How can we help you with?”.
Page: Dashboard - Get Started
A new page, Get started, is added to the user dashboard. It will display a relevant information based on a selected onboarding step.
Design Validation
Deliverables approached for validating the designs
We conducted 2 rounds of remote focus group to test the onboarding user experience in the mobile app from mid to high fidelity wireframes.
The objectives were to gain the insights about the following:
Awareness/Education created through the Onboarding experience
Identifying the effectiveness of the prototype
Demonstrating a usability score rating the level of engagement with the prototype and the ease of navigation through the slides
Understanding what resonates with the members/ their likes and dislikes
Identifying challenges (if any) and iterating wireframes to mitigate them
Drop off points
Clarity with respect to the articulated directions/ terminology
Results
A summary of high level insights from the design validation
Most participants thought that the tutorial was easy to understand and straight forward.
The participants expressed that they felt aware and confident about engaging with the Flybuys program after going through the app tour.
The pre-registration carousel is impactful.

Users and their needs, level of awareness are subjective. Results from phase 2 of testing further solidify the findings from stage one that highlighted that members may or may not want to go through the whole tutorial. They would prefer to have access to the tutorial if and when they want it.
Post-launch
We implemented the new onboarding experience and saw an increase of 12% increase in downloads of the mobile app.
We saw a 25% increase in members adding a digital card to their wallet.
Overall mobile app engagement increased by 15% as a result and encouraged further investment within the acquisition and onboarding part of the customer journey.
Flybuys Case Studies
Flybuys Mobile Vision and high fidelity concepts
